spec_match.rationale: The histogram now samples the backend route for ctx and no-ctx registrations, so it demonstrates the real emitted header alongside the cap math. DevTools MCP verified v134, pre-v134, and mixed modes.

interactive.rationale: Conversions-per-source and sources/day sliders plus three mode buttons update the histogram, attempted/reported/dropped stats, backend route status, and header preview.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page promises a heavy-flow probe with a live histogram and dropped-event counter. It now also shows the actual backend header sample for the selected regime.

edge_cases.rationale: Mixed migration is represented with a 50 percent ctx_id mode, and low-volume mixed traffic below the legacy cap correctly reports zero drops.

references.rationale: Includes the feature index, sibling concepts, trigger_context_id spec anchor, and ChromeStatus feature link.

design_system.rationale: The stat foreground now uses design-system variables instead of literal white, and the page has no document-level horizontal overflow under 390px mobile emulation.

summary: Re-reviewed in Chrome 148 through chrome-devtools-mcp. Verified default v134 mode reports 200/200 with 0 dropped, pre-v134 reports 100/200 with 100 dropped, mixed mode reports 160/200 with 40 dropped, and mixed low-volume 10x4 reports 40/40 with 0 dropped. Network requests to the backend route were 200; console only showed expected localhost Attribution Reporting attestation warnings.
